name: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
window: Q1 FY26 · May 06 → Jul 15 2026 (into the Jul-23 'Advancing AI' event)

events.text: Q1 FY26: rev $10.3B (+38% y/y, beat at the top of guide), EPS $1.37 (+43% y/y), non-GAAP GM 55% (+170bps y/y), op income $2.5B (25% OM), record FCF $2.6B. Record Data Center $5.8B (+57% y/y); Server CPU 4th consecutive record, >50% y/y. Q2 guide ~$11.2B ±$300M (+46% y/y), GM ~56%, Server CPU guided >70% y/y.

Server CPU >70% y/y in Q2, sustainable through 2H, mostly unit-driven (~2/3 units, 1/3 ASP) at 60-65%+ GM vs lower-margin GPU+HBM. Each GW of deal ≈ $15-20B; OpenAI + Meta ≈ 2GW of the ~3GW in play for 2027. AMD confirms ~$15B+ per GW and 'multiple GW+/year customers'. The final OpenAI tranche only vests at $600/share; ~26M shares per GW of dilution via warrant.

Post-Q1 recap: consensus moving to ~$7.25 EPS FY26 (from ~$6.80), ~$12+ FY27, ~$15 FY28; AMD historically trades 35-40x next-year in growth → target $450-500. AMD remains under-owned — only ~1-in-3 large-cap funds hold it at ~0.41x relative weight (~36-60% underweight) vs NVDA ~78% ownership — the positioning gap is itself the bull catalyst.
GS upgraded to Buy PT $410; part of the broad sell-side reset post-Q1 on the agentic CPU thesis (BofA Buy $450, MS OW $410, UBS Buy $300).
Asia recap: MI450 'on track' for first revenue in Q4; Curtis saw 2 of the 3 MI450 ODMs (end-customers Meta and Microsoft) and flagged Inventec building MI450 for Microsoft as a new data point.
Citi upgraded to Buy, PT $575, ~+12-13% above the Street CY26-28E, SOTP-based — sees 2027 GPU revenue dispersion of $30-50bn as ~3GW come into play.
JPM Neutral PT $385 vs stock ~$511 — explicitly cautious on valuation (~45x NTM); GPU still 'show-me'.

Asked AMD-vs-Intel-vs-ARM, Arcuri 'would rather be in AMD' — core count is decisive in an agentic world (agents spawning agents), and AMD is 'well ahead of Intel on core count'; much less optimistic on Intel's product roadmap. AMD server units +50%.

AMD DC capex at $35-40B per GW, of which AMD revenue is $15-20B/GW ($14-18B compute, ~$2B networking); raises GPU units to 0.7M (2025) → 0.6M (2026E) → 1.0M (2027E) as MI450 ramps; OpenAI deal (6 GW MI450, multi-generation) sees first ~1 GW deploy 2H26.

Bus tour: 'very enthused' on Venice (2nm, 2H ramp), sees agentic CPU as incremental net-new demand, not a GPU ratio, and flags x86 security advantage for enterprise agents over Arm. On GPU 'still show-me' with the stock at ~45x next year.
Bernstein Outperform PT $600.

Asia Trip Takeaways: AMD constrained on TSMC 3nm + two substrate bottlenecks — ABF (Kinsus ~40% share, growing only ~10%) and CCL (iTeq ~60% share at ~75% util on a T-glass shortage, recovering to 90-95% in Q4). Trade: if long AMD, trim ~20% into the print since AMD may guide light on the constraints, then buy the weakness. Server CPU pricing +15-20% again in H2.

MI450/Helios: MI450 chip in production on track; rack forecast steady at ~30,000 units for next year (mostly Meta and OpenAI), via AVC (liquid cooling)/Delta (power) checks. ODMs split on Helios rack assembly timing — one sees a delay to Q1 2026, another only a minor slip to late Q4 2025; the swing is whether the rack (not the chip) can be built. Vs NVDA's ~70-80K racks, 30K is a 'decent number'.

'Revisiting AMD and ARM Estimates' — Buy, PT raised to $670 (from $455), 35x. EPS C26/27/28 $7.61/$13.47/$18.85; rev $50.4B/$79.2B/$104B. Lifts AMD C2030 server-CPU revenue to $50B (from $41B) in a $170B C2030 CPU TAM (units 3M→33M AI-server CPUs); standalone agentic CPU racks skew x86 60/40 (AMD the winner on core count/throughput); AMD server-CPU C26/27/28 rev $16B/$23B/$29B.

'DRAM Supply Is the New Ranking Metric' — AMD ranked #1 among large caps. With DRAM the biggest constraint on AI compute (AWS EC2 GPU +20% price hike; AI undersupplied), AMD is served by BOTH Samsung and Micron (dual-sourced) — a favorable supply-security read vs peers (QCOM ranked last).

AMD Venice server-CPU volumes estimated at 4mn units in 2027, up from 1.3mn in 2026; AMD's total CoWoS demand could grow 232% in 2027E on the Venice ramp plus MI450/455 — a materially steeper unit/CoWoS ramp than prior framings implied.

Semis 2Q Preview: Buy, PT raised to $640 (from $450) (32x normalized EPS $20.00); 2027 EPS est $14.50, 13% above Street; expects a strong 2Q + 3Q guide on server-CPU demand + MI450 2H ramp. Names AMD among tactical UPSIDE ideas into 2Q (with AMAT, ON), vs downside KLAC/ARM/QCOM.

'DC Capex Ramping to >$1.4T in 2028E' — AMD Outperform, PT $615 (~42x F27E EPS; bull $800 / bear $450), first Mizuho mark on the page. AMD a technology-enabler of AI-DC-capex ramping to $1.2T (2027E) / >$1.4T (2028E) (~$50B/GW); the 'credible second-place AI accelerator' into a ~$1T-by-2030 accelerator TAM.

'Core Wars Q2 Preview' — Buy, PO raised $550 → $620 (47x CY27E PE). Expects a beat/raise on EPYC server-CPU share gains + strong cloud; Q3 likely to incorporate the first MI455X 'Helios' rack shipment → ~$6-7bn+/qtr exiting Q4, with 6th-gen EPYC Venice alongside; Jul-23 'Advancing AI 2026' the catalyst. Venice 256c = 3.30x rack-geomean lead over NVDA Vera; ~25-27% server-CPU value share through CY30.

KeyBanc raises general-purpose server-CPU unit-growth outlook to +30% (from +18.5%) as AMD/INTC squeeze out additional wafer supply. AMD obtained incremental N4 wafers at TSMC (MediaTek smartphone cuts) → +15-20% AMD server-CPU unit growth this year; AMD and INTC both target >50% server-CPU unit growth in 2027 (units, additive to mgmt's >70% Q2 revenue growth).

'Previewing AMD's AI Day' — Buy, PT raised to $700 (from $670), 33x on C28E EPS $21.11. EPS C26/27/28 $7.69/$14.63/$21.11; rev $50.7B/$83.4B/$112.3B; GPU rev C27 $41B (from $38B), C28 $61B (from $54B) on ~$15-20B/GW × three ~1GW+ customers by 2027. Amazon a major MI450x customer, Anthropic possibly on the list; possible AMD-Cerebras fast-inference partnership + deeper custom-ASIC push.

cells: all-time high, Turin ~½ of EPYC rev
signif. dbl-digit y/y; MI350 mid-yr ramp
declined y/y on China; MI350 launched
grew y/y; sharp MI350 ramp; OpenAI 6GW
dbl-digit y/y; Meta 6GW; MI450 ramps H2
MI308 to China ~eliminated; licenses in review
some licenses received; none in Q4 guide
